activeGoal: Preserve transcript-relevant OpenCode server information across the Python adapter to extension boundary so the extensio
designSummary: ELM-006 remediation is a structural application-layer refinement across the Python adapter, shared extension protocol, transcript projection mapper, and conversation renderer. The adapter must preserve transcript-relevant message identity, part identity, channel semantics, emission kind, and compact tool metadata across the NormalizedRunEvent boundary. The extension contract must expose those semantics without promoting all preserved metadata to visible UI content. The projection mapper becomes the single authority for transcript assembly: TOOL items remain internally available but are suppressed from direct conversation rendering, while TEXT-bearing assistant items are merged by stable logical message identity so one assistant message renders once. Existing modules provide the correct ownership boundaries; no new software unit is required.
designDecisions: ADR-ELM-006-01: Extend existing software units instead of creating a new transcript module. Rationale: python_adapter/app/opencode_adapter.py and extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts already own normalization and projection semantics, so adding a new unit would duplicate existing boundaries rather than improve separation of concerns.

ADR-ELM-006-02: Use semantic.messageId as the primary logical assistant-message anchor and semantic.partId as the secondary chunk/part anchor. Rationale: current projection logic already partially relies on message identity, but deterministic one-message rendering requires the adapter contract to preserve stable server-side logical message identity across delta, snapshot, and final emissions.

ADR-ELM-006-03: Preserve TOOL metadata across the adapter boundary but suppress TOOL items from direct conversation rendering. Rationale: the requirement forbids direct TOOL display in the conversation UI, but transcript/session assembly and future diagnostics still need compact tool state and identity metadata.

ADR-ELM-006-04: Keep transcript grouping and suppression policy inside the Reasoning Projection Mapper, not inside JSX rendering components. Rationale: grouping by logical message identity and hiding TOOL items are application behavior concerns; the renderer should consume the projected read-model without reinterpreting raw event semantics.

issues.summary: IntentNotVerified for transcript stream contract audit
issues.details: IntentNotVerified for commit 35358ba3cec0e2661102a105fc0005fb93e1feea. Missing verified intended IDs: ELM-APP-EXT-SHARED-API-CONTRACT. Reviewed task IDs: TASK-105, TASK-106, TASK-107, TASK-108. Existing tagged automated tests verify ELM-APP-008C, ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER, and ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER, but no tagged automated test verifies ELM-APP-EXT-SHARED-API-CONTRACT. QA must add tagged automated tests before release can continue.

qa.summary: QA passed for commit 35358ba3cec0e2661102a105fc0005fb93e1feea. Covered architecture IDs: ELM-APP-008C, ELM-APP-EXT-SHARED-API-CONTRACT, ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER, ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER. Added missing traceability tags to python_adapter/tests/test_opencode_adapter.py for adapter coverage, then ran targeted pytest and extension vitest suites plus extension typecheck/build. No functional defects found in transcript semantic preservation, TOOL suppression, logical-message text merging, or projected-transcript rendering authority.

audit.summary: Re-audit passed for commit b487a2fd09499b10d7a52e9bf1f9a4c4f6f853a6. TASK-106 adds tagged automated test coverage in extension/src/shared/api.test.ts for ELM-APP-EXT-SHARED-API-CONTRACT, closing the prior IntentNotVerified gap. The shared contract test verifies semantic and compact tool metadata parsing, and the broader as-built transcript remediation remains aligned with approved architecture across adapter, mapper, and renderer. Minor scanner normalization artifacts remain for TS block-comment tags with trailing */, but they are non-blocking for this re-audit.
